seo.title: Privacy Policy
seo.description: Privacy Policy for SCIT Consulting. Learn how we collect, use, store, and protect your personal data in full compliance with the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and applicable Estonian and European data protection laws.

sections.heading: 1. Introduction
sections.body:
SCIT Consulting ("we", "us", "our") is committed to protecting your privacy and ensuring the security of your personal data. This Privacy Policy explains in detail how we collect, use, store, share, and protect the personal information you provide when visiting our website at https://scitconsulting.eu, using our interactive tools, communicating with our AI chatbot, booking consultations, or otherwise interacting with our services.

This policy applies to all visitors, users, and clients of our website and services, regardless of their location. We process personal data in compliance with the General Data Protection Regulation (EU) 2016/679 ("GDPR"), the Estonian Personal Data Protection Act (Isikuandmete kaitse seadus), and other applicable European data protection legislation.

By using our website and services, you acknowledge that you have read and understood this Privacy Policy. We encourage you to read it carefully. If you have any questions about our data practices, please contact us at privacy@scitconsulting.eu before using our services.

This document serves as the legally required privacy notice under Articles 13 and 14 of the GDPR. It is designed to be transparent, comprehensive, and accessible, in accordance with the principle of transparency set out in Article 5(1)(a) of the GDPR.

sections.heading: 2. Data Controller
sections.body:
The data controller responsible for processing your personal data is SCIT Consulting, a company registered and operating in Tallinn, Estonia, within the European Union.

As the data controller, SCIT Consulting determines the purposes and means of the processing of your personal data. We take this responsibility seriously and have implemented appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure that processing is performed in accordance with the GDPR.

For all privacy-related inquiries, requests to exercise your data protection rights, or complaints regarding our data processing activities, you may contact us at: privacy@scitconsulting.eu. For general business inquiries, you may reach us at: hello@scitconsulting.eu. We aim to respond to all privacy-related requests within 30 days of receipt, in accordance with Article 12(3) of the GDPR.

Given the nature and scale of our data processing activities (a small consultancy of 2 to 10 employees processing limited categories of personal data for clearly defined purposes), we are not required under Article 37 of the GDPR to appoint a Data Protection Officer (DPO). Nevertheless, our privacy contact at privacy@scitconsulting.eu serves as the designated point of contact for all data protection matters.

sections.heading: 3. Information We Collect
sections.body:
We collect and process different categories of personal data depending on how you interact with our website and services. We are committed to the principle of data minimisation under Article 5(1)(c) of the GDPR, meaning we only collect data that is necessary for the specific purpose at hand.

Contact Form Data: When you submit our contact form, we collect your full name, email address, phone number (optional), and the content of your message. This form is protected by Cloudflare Turnstile, which generates a token to verify you are a human user. Cloudflare Turnstile does not use cookies or collect personal data beyond what is necessary for bot detection.

AI Readiness Assessment: When you use our AI Readiness Assessment tool, we collect your answers to the assessment questions. Optionally, if you choose to receive a detailed report, you may provide your email address and company name. You may also skip the email step entirely and view your results without providing any personal information.

ROI Calculator: Our ROI Calculator is a fully client-side tool that performs calculations locally in your browser. It does not collect, transmit, or store any personal data.

Chatbot Conversations: Our website features an AI-powered chatbot provided by Botpress. When you interact with the chatbot, the content of your conversation is processed by Botpress to generate responses. We advise you not to share sensitive personal information, financial details, or confidential data through the chatbot.

Booking Data: When you schedule a consultation through our Cal.com (cal.eu) booking widget, Cal.com collects your name, email address, and any additional information you provide during the booking process. This data is processed by Cal.com in accordance with their own privacy policy, and a copy of relevant booking details is shared with us to facilitate the scheduled consultation.

Technical and Server Data: Our website is hosted on Cloudflare Pages. Cloudflare may automatically collect certain technical data such as your IP address, browser type, operating system, referring URL, and access timestamps for the purposes of delivering the website, providing security (including DDoS protection), and maintaining performance. This data is processed by Cloudflare as a data processor on our behalf.

Font Data: Our website uses Google Fonts to load the Inter typeface. When you visit our website, your browser makes a request to Google servers to retrieve the font files. This request may transmit your IP address and browser information to Google. We use Google Fonts to ensure consistent typography across all devices.

sections.heading: 4. Legal Basis for Processing
sections.body:
Under Article 6 of the GDPR, we must have a valid legal basis for each processing activity involving your personal data. We rely on the following legal bases depending on the context and purpose of the processing.

Consent (Article 6(1)(a) GDPR): We rely on your freely given, specific, informed, and unambiguous consent when you voluntarily submit your contact details through our contact form, when you optionally provide your email address in the AI Readiness Assessment to receive results, and when you initiate a conversation with our chatbot. You have the right to withdraw your consent at any time by contacting us at privacy@scitconsulting.eu. Withdrawal of consent does not affect the lawfulness of processing carried out prior to the withdrawal.

Performance of a Contract or Pre-contractual Measures (Article 6(1)(b) GDPR): We process personal data when it is necessary for the performance of a contract to which you are a party, or in order to take steps at your request prior to entering into a contract. This applies when you book a consultation through Cal.com, when we respond to your enquiries about our consulting services, and when we deliver services you have engaged us to provide.

Legitimate Interests (Article 6(1)(f) GDPR): We rely on our legitimate interests for processing that is necessary to operate, secure, and improve our website and services, provided that such interests are not overridden by your fundamental rights and freedoms. This includes maintaining the security and integrity of our website through Cloudflare, analysing aggregated and anonymised usage patterns to improve our services, and communicating with you in response to enquiries you have initiated. We have conducted a legitimate interest assessment for each of these activities and concluded that our interests do not override your rights, particularly given the limited nature and low sensitivity of the data involved.

sections.heading: 5. How We Use Your Information
sections.body:
We use the personal data we collect for specific, explicit, and legitimate purposes as outlined below. We do not process your personal data in any manner that is incompatible with these stated purposes, in accordance with the purpose limitation principle under Article 5(1)(b) of the GDPR.

Responding to Enquiries: When you submit a message through our contact form, we use your name, email address, and message content to understand your needs and respond to your enquiry in a timely manner. If you provided a phone number, we may use it to contact you if email communication proves insufficient.

Delivering Consultation Services: When you book a consultation through our Cal.com widget, we use your booking information to prepare for and conduct the scheduled meeting, send you confirmation and reminder communications, and follow up after the consultation if appropriate.

Providing Assessment Results: If you complete the AI Readiness Assessment and choose to provide your email, we use it to deliver your personalised assessment report. Your assessment answers are used to generate a readiness score and tailored recommendations.

Improving Our Website and Services: We use aggregated, non-identifying technical data to understand how visitors interact with our website, identify and resolve technical issues, and improve the overall user experience.

Ensuring Security: We use Cloudflare services, including Turnstile, to protect our website from malicious traffic, automated abuse, spam submissions, and denial-of-service attacks.

We do not use your personal data for automated decision-making or profiling that produces legal effects or similarly significant effects on you, as described in Article 22 of the GDPR. We do not sell your personal data to any third party. We do not use your personal data for advertising, behavioural tracking, or marketing purposes beyond responding to communications you have initiated.

sections.heading: 6. Data Sharing and Third Parties
sections.body:
We do not sell, rent, or trade your personal data to third parties. We share your personal data only with the following carefully selected service providers who process data on our behalf (as data processors under Article 28 of the GDPR) or as independent controllers for the purposes described below.

Cloudflare, Inc.: Cloudflare provides our website hosting (Cloudflare Pages), content delivery network (CDN), DDoS protection, and the Turnstile anti-bot verification service. Cloudflare processes technical data (such as IP addresses and request metadata) to deliver, secure, and optimise our website. Cloudflare is certified under the EU-US Data Privacy Framework. For more information, please refer to Cloudflare's privacy policy at https://www.cloudflare.com/privacypolicy/.

Resend, Inc.: Resend is used as our transactional email delivery service. When you submit the contact form, Resend processes your name and email address to deliver our response from noreply@scitconsulting.eu. Resend acts as a data processor and processes data only as instructed by us. For more information, refer to Resend's privacy policy at https://resend.com/legal/privacy-policy.

Cal.com, Inc.: Cal.com (operating at cal.eu for European users) provides our consultation booking functionality. When you book a consultation, Cal.com processes your name, email, and booking details as an independent data controller for the scheduling service, and shares relevant booking information with us. For more information, refer to Cal.com's privacy policy at https://cal.com/privacy.

Botpress (Botpress, Inc.): Botpress provides the AI chatbot widget on our website. When you interact with the chatbot, your conversation content is sent to Botpress for processing and response generation. Botpress may use AI language models (including third-party models) to generate replies. For more information, refer to Botpress's privacy policy at https://botpress.com/privacy.

Google LLC (Google Fonts): Our website loads the Inter font from Google Fonts servers. When the font is requested, Google receives your IP address and browser information. Google Fonts does not set cookies. For more information, refer to Google's privacy policy at https://policies.google.com/privacy.

We require all data processors to implement appropriate technical and organisational security measures and to process personal data only in accordance with our documented instructions. We may also disclose your personal data if required to do so by law, regulation, legal process, or governmental request.

sections.heading: 7. International Data Transfers
sections.body:
SCIT Consulting is headquartered in Tallinn, Estonia, within the European Union. Our primary website hosting through Cloudflare Pages utilises a global content delivery network, which means that website content may be served from Cloudflare edge servers located in various countries, including locations outside the European Economic Area (EEA).

Where personal data is transferred to countries outside the EEA that have not received an adequacy decision from the European Commission, we ensure that appropriate safeguards are in place in accordance with Chapter V of the GDPR. These safeguards include the following mechanisms.

EU-US Data Privacy Framework: Cloudflare, Inc. and other US-based service providers we use have certified their compliance with the EU-US Data Privacy Framework (DPF), providing an adequate level of protection for personal data transferred from the EU to the United States, as recognised by the European Commission's adequacy decision of 10 July 2023.

Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs): Where the Data Privacy Framework does not apply, we rely on the European Commission's Standard Contractual Clauses (adopted under Commission Implementing Decision (EU) 2021/914) to provide appropriate safeguards for the transfer of personal data to third countries. These clauses are incorporated into our agreements with relevant service providers.

Supplementary Measures: Where necessary, we implement supplementary technical and organisational measures (such as encryption in transit and at rest) to ensure that transferred data receives an essentially equivalent level of protection as within the EEA, in line with the guidance from the European Data Protection Board (EDPB).

You may obtain a copy of the safeguards we have put in place for international data transfers by contacting us at privacy@scitconsulting.eu.

sections.heading: 8. Data Retention
sections.body:
We retain your personal data only for as long as is necessary to fulfil the purposes for which it was collected, in accordance with the storage limitation principle under Article 5(1)(e) of the GDPR. The specific retention periods depend on the category of data and the purpose of processing.

Contact Form Submissions: We retain the data you submit through the contact form (name, email, phone number, and message) for a maximum of 12 months from the date of submission, or for the duration of any resulting business relationship, whichever is longer. If your enquiry does not lead to a business engagement, we delete or anonymise your data after 12 months.

AI Readiness Assessment Data: If you provide your email address and company name during the assessment, we retain this data together with your assessment results for a maximum of 12 months. Assessment answers submitted without personal identifiers are retained in anonymised form for analytical purposes.

Booking Data: Consultation booking data processed through Cal.com is retained for the duration necessary to conduct and follow up on the consultation, and thereafter for up to 12 months. Cal.com retains data according to its own retention policies.

Chatbot Conversation Data: Conversations with our chatbot are processed by Botpress and are retained in accordance with Botpress's data retention policies. We do not independently store chatbot conversation logs.

Technical and Server Logs: Cloudflare retains server logs and technical data in accordance with its own retention policies, typically for a limited period necessary for security and performance purposes.

When the retention period expires, we securely delete or irreversibly anonymise the personal data. If deletion is not immediately possible (for example, because the data has been archived in backup systems), we ensure that the data is isolated from further processing until deletion is carried out.

sections.heading: 9. Cookies and Tracking Technologies
sections.body:
Our website uses only essential cookies that are strictly necessary for the proper functioning of the website. We do not use advertising cookies, tracking pixels, social media cookies, or any third-party analytics cookies.

Essential cookies may include those set by Cloudflare for security purposes (such as the __cf_bm cookie for bot management) and any session cookies necessary for core website functionality. These cookies are exempt from the consent requirement under Article 5(3) of the ePrivacy Directive (2002/58/EC), as they are strictly necessary for the provision of the service explicitly requested by the user.

Cloudflare Turnstile, which we use to protect our contact form from automated abuse, operates without placing tracking cookies in the user's browser. It uses non-invasive signals to distinguish between human users and bots.

Our AI chatbot widget provided by Botpress may use local storage or session storage in your browser to maintain conversation context during your browsing session. This data is temporary and is cleared when you close your browser.

We do not use Google Analytics, Facebook Pixel, or any similar tracking or advertising technology. We do not engage in cross-site tracking, behavioural advertising, or retargeting. We do not build user profiles based on browsing behaviour.

Because we only use strictly necessary cookies that are exempt under the ePrivacy Directive, we do not display a cookie consent banner requesting your permission for cookies. If our cookie practices change in the future to include non-essential cookies, we will implement an appropriate consent mechanism before any such cookies are placed.

sections.heading: 10. Your Rights Under the GDPR
sections.body:
As a data subject, you have the following rights under the GDPR with respect to the personal data we hold about you. These rights apply regardless of your nationality or place of residence, as we extend GDPR protections to all users of our website.

Right of Access (Article 15): You have the right to request confirmation as to whether we process your personal data and, if so, to obtain a copy of that data along with information about how and why it is processed.

Right to Rectification (Article 16): You have the right to request the correction of inaccurate personal data or the completion of incomplete personal data that we hold about you.

Right to Erasure (Article 17): You have the right to request the deletion of your personal data where there is no compelling reason for its continued processing, for example when the data is no longer necessary for the original purpose, or you withdraw your consent.

Right to Restriction of Processing (Article 18): You have the right to request that we restrict the processing of your personal data in certain circumstances, such as when you contest the accuracy of the data or object to our processing.

Right to Data Portability (Article 20): You have the right to receive the personal data you have provided to us in a structured, commonly used, and machine-readable format, and to transmit that data to another controller without hindrance.

Right to Object (Article 21): You have the right to object to the processing of your personal data where we rely on legitimate interests as the legal basis. We will cease processing unless we can demonstrate compelling legitimate grounds that override your interests, rights, and freedoms.

Right to Withdraw Consent (Article 7(3)): Where we process your personal data based on your consent, you have the right to withdraw that consent at any time. Withdrawal of consent does not affect the lawfulness of any processing carried out before the withdrawal.

Right to Lodge a Complaint: If you believe that our processing of your personal data infringes the GDPR, you have the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority. The competent supervisory authority for SCIT Consulting is the Estonian Data Protection Inspectorate (Andmekaitse Inspektsioon), located at Tatari 39, 10134 Tallinn, Estonia, reachable at info@aki.ee. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the supervisory authority in the EU Member State of your habitual residence, place of work, or place of the alleged infringement.

To exercise any of these rights, please contact us at privacy@scitconsulting.eu. We will respond to your request without undue delay and in any event within one month of receipt. If your request is particularly complex or we have received a large number of requests, we may extend this period by a further two months, in which case we will inform you accordingly. There is no fee for exercising your rights, unless requests are manifestly unfounded or excessive.

sections.heading: 11. Children's Privacy
sections.body:
Our website and services are not directed at children. We provide business-to-business AI consulting, automation, and digital transformation services that are designed for professionals, business owners, and organisational decision-makers.

We do not knowingly collect or process personal data from children under the age of 16, which is the age threshold established under Article 8 of the GDPR as applied in Estonia. If we become aware that we have inadvertently collected personal data from a child under the age of 16 without verified parental consent, we will take immediate steps to delete such data from our records.

If you are a parent or guardian and believe that your child has provided personal data to us, please contact us immediately at privacy@scitconsulting.eu so that we can take the necessary steps to remove the information.

sections.heading: 12. Changes to This Policy
sections.body:
We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in our data processing practices, the services we offer, legal or regulatory requirements, or other operational, legal, or regulatory reasons.

When we make changes to this Privacy Policy, we will update the "Last updated" date at the top of this page. For significant changes that materially affect how we process your personal data or your rights, we will take reasonable steps to notify you, such as displaying a prominent notice on our website or, where we hold your email address and have a legal basis to contact you, sending you a direct notification.

We encourage you to review this Privacy Policy periodically to stay informed about how we protect your personal data. Your continued use of our website and services after any changes to this Privacy Policy constitutes your acknowledgement of those changes.

Previous versions of this Privacy Policy are available upon request by contacting us at privacy@scitconsulting.eu.

sections.heading: 13. Contact Us
sections.body:
If you have any questions, concerns, or requests regarding this Privacy Policy, our data processing practices, or your personal data rights, please do not hesitate to contact us.

SCIT Consulting
Tallinn, Estonia
European Union

Privacy inquiries: privacy@scitconsulting.eu
General inquiries: hello@scitconsulting.eu
Website: https://scitconsulting.eu

We aim to resolve all privacy-related concerns and requests promptly. If you are not satisfied with our response, you have the right to lodge a complaint with the Estonian Data Protection Inspectorate (Andmekaitse Inspektsioon) at info@aki.ee, or with the supervisory authority in your country of residence.

seo.title: Terms of Service
seo.description: Terms of Service for SCIT Consulting. Read our terms governing the use of our website, AI consulting services, and digital transformation solutions. Governed by the laws of the Republic of Estonia.

sections.heading: 1. Introduction & Acceptance of Terms
sections.body:
Welcome to SCIT Consulting. These Terms of Service ("Terms") constitute a legally binding agreement between you ("User", "you", "your") and SCIT Consulting OU ("SCIT Consulting", "Company", "we", "us", "our"), a company registered in the Republic of Estonia with its headquarters in Tallinn, Estonia, operating the website at https://scitconsulting.eu (the "Website").

By accessing or using our Website, booking a consultation, engaging our services, or interacting with any of our online tools or features, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms in their entirety. If you do not agree to these Terms, you must not access or use the Website or our services.

These Terms apply to all visitors, users, clients, and any other persons who access or use the Website. Where you are acting on behalf of a business entity, you represent and warrant that you have the authority to bind that entity to these Terms, and all references to "you" shall include that entity.

These Terms are supplemented by our Privacy Policy, which is incorporated herein by reference. For individual consulting engagements, separate service agreements or statements of work may apply, and in the event of a conflict between such agreements and these Terms, the provisions of the individual service agreement shall prevail to the extent of the inconsistency.

sections.heading: 2. Description of Services
sections.body:
SCIT Consulting specialises in artificial intelligence consulting, implementation, and digital transformation services. Our service offerings include, but are not limited to: AI Automation and Process Automation, Custom AI Agents, Chatbots and Conversational AI, AI Tools Implementation (including platforms such as ChatGPT Enterprise, Claude, GitHub Copilot, and similar technologies), AI Strategy and Consulting, and Digital Transformation advisory services.

Through our Website, we provide informational content about our services, educational blog articles, interactive tools such as our ROI Calculator and AI Readiness Assessment, an AI-powered chatbot for general inquiries, a contact form for business inquiries, and consultation booking functionality powered by Cal.com.

The specific scope, deliverables, timelines, and fees for consulting engagements are defined in individual service agreements or statements of work entered into between SCIT Consulting and the client. These Terms govern the general relationship and use of the Website; they do not, by themselves, create a consulting engagement or obligation to provide services.

We reserve the right to modify, suspend, or discontinue any aspect of our services or Website features at any time without prior notice, except where such changes materially affect an existing service agreement.

sections.heading: 3. Use of Website
sections.body:
You may use our Website solely for lawful purposes and in accordance with these Terms. You agree to use the Website only for its intended purposes, which include obtaining information about our services, contacting us for business inquiries, booking consultations, and using the informational tools provided.

You agree not to: use the Website in any way that violates any applicable local, national, or international law or regulation, including but not limited to European Union regulations; attempt to gain unauthorised access to any portion of the Website, its servers, or any systems or networks connected to the Website; use any automated system, including bots, scrapers, or crawlers, to access the Website for any purpose without our express written permission; introduce any viruses, trojans, worms, logic bombs, or other material that is malicious or technologically harmful; impersonate or attempt to impersonate SCIT Consulting, an employee, another user, or any other person or entity; or use the Website to transmit any unsolicited advertising, promotional materials, or spam.

We reserve the right to restrict or terminate your access to the Website at any time, without notice, if we reasonably believe you have violated these Terms or any applicable law. Any such restriction or termination shall be without prejudice to any other rights or remedies available to us.

sections.heading: 4. Consultation Bookings
sections.body:
Our Website allows you to schedule consultations through our booking system, which is powered by Cal.com, a third-party scheduling platform. By booking a consultation, you agree to provide accurate contact and scheduling information and to honour your scheduled appointment.

Consultation slots are subject to availability. We make reasonable efforts to accommodate your preferred time, but we do not guarantee the availability of any particular slot. Confirmation of a booking does not constitute a binding service agreement; it is an arrangement for an initial discussion.

If you need to cancel or reschedule a consultation, we ask that you do so at least 24 hours before the scheduled time. Repeated no-shows or last-minute cancellations without reasonable cause may result in restrictions on future booking privileges. We understand that unforeseen circumstances arise and will assess such situations on a case-by-case basis.

SCIT Consulting reserves the right to cancel or reschedule consultations at any time due to unforeseen circumstances or scheduling conflicts. In such cases, we will make reasonable efforts to notify you promptly and offer an alternative time.

sections.heading: 5. Intellectual Property
sections.body:
All content on this Website, including but not limited to text, graphics, logos, icons, images, audio clips, digital downloads, data compilations, software, and the design, selection, and arrangement thereof, is the exclusive property of SCIT Consulting or its content suppliers and is protected by Estonian, European Union, and international intellectual property laws, including copyright, trademark, and trade dress laws.

You may not reproduce, distribute, modify, create derivative works of, publicly display, publicly perform, republish, download, store, or transmit any of the material on our Website without our prior written consent, except that you may print or download one copy of a reasonable number of pages for your own personal, non-commercial, informational use, provided that you do not modify the content and that you retain all copyright and other proprietary notices.

With respect to consulting engagements, unless otherwise specified in a separate service agreement, the following shall apply: all deliverables created by SCIT Consulting for a client in the course of a consulting engagement shall be owned by the client upon receipt of full payment for that engagement. Until such payment is received in full, SCIT Consulting retains all rights, title, and interest in and to the deliverables. SCIT Consulting retains the right to use general knowledge, skills, experience, ideas, concepts, know-how, and techniques acquired or developed during any engagement.

Any pre-existing intellectual property, frameworks, tools, templates, or methodologies owned by SCIT Consulting prior to or independently of a client engagement remain the sole property of SCIT Consulting. Where such pre-existing materials are incorporated into client deliverables, SCIT Consulting grants the client a non-exclusive, non-transferable, perpetual licence to use them solely as part of the deliverables.

sections.heading: 6. AI-Generated Content Disclaimer
sections.body:
Our Website includes interactive tools, such as the ROI Calculator and the AI Readiness Assessment, which are provided strictly for informational and educational purposes. The results, projections, estimates, scores, and recommendations generated by these tools are approximate in nature and are based on generalised models and assumptions. They do not constitute professional advice, financial projections, performance guarantees, or promises of specific outcomes.

You acknowledge and agree that: the outputs of these tools are estimates only and may not reflect your actual results, savings, or return on investment; the accuracy of any output depends on the accuracy and completeness of the information you provide; these tools do not take into account all variables, circumstances, or factors that may affect your specific situation; and no reliance should be placed on the outputs of these tools as the sole basis for any business decision.

SCIT Consulting expressly disclaims all liability arising from or related to your use of or reliance on the outputs of these tools. For tailored advice based on your specific circumstances, we recommend booking a consultation with our team. Any actual projections or analyses provided as part of a paid consulting engagement will be subject to the terms and conditions of the applicable service agreement.

sections.heading: 7. Chatbot Disclaimer
sections.body:
Our Website features an AI-powered chatbot (powered by Botpress) designed to assist visitors by providing general information about SCIT Consulting, our services, and related topics. The chatbot uses artificial intelligence to generate responses and may not always provide perfectly accurate, complete, or up-to-date information.

The information provided by the chatbot is for general informational purposes only and does not constitute professional consulting advice, legal advice, financial advice, or any other form of professional recommendation. The chatbot is not a substitute for a personalised consultation with a qualified member of our team.

SCIT Consulting does not guarantee the accuracy, reliability, or completeness of any information provided by the chatbot. You should not rely solely on the chatbot's responses when making business decisions. We recommend verifying any critical information directly with our team by contacting us at hello@scitconsulting.eu or by booking a consultation.

By using the chatbot, you acknowledge that your interactions may be processed and stored in accordance with our Privacy Policy and the privacy practices of Botpress as a third-party service provider.

sections.heading: 8. Confidentiality
sections.body:
Both SCIT Consulting and the User acknowledge that, in the course of any interaction, consultation, or engagement, either party may disclose or make available to the other party certain information that is confidential or proprietary in nature ("Confidential Information"). Confidential Information includes, but is not limited to, business plans, strategies, technical information, financial data, client lists, proprietary methodologies, and any information designated as confidential or that a reasonable person would understand to be confidential given the nature of the information and circumstances of disclosure.

Each party agrees to: hold the other party's Confidential Information in strict confidence; not disclose Confidential Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the disclosing party; use Confidential Information solely for the purpose for which it was disclosed; and take reasonable measures to protect the confidentiality of such information, using at least the same degree of care it uses to protect its own confidential information.

These confidentiality obligations do not apply to information that: is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the receiving party; was already known to the receiving party prior to disclosure; is independently developed by the receiving party without reference to the Confidential Information; or is required to be disclosed by law, regulation, or court order, provided that the receiving party gives the disclosing party prompt written notice and cooperates in any effort to obtain protective treatment.

These confidentiality obligations survive the termination of any engagement or these Terms for a period of three (3) years following such termination.

sections.heading: 9. Limitation of Liability
sections.body:
To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, including the laws of the Republic of Estonia and the European Union, SCIT Consulting, its directors, employees, partners, agents, and affiliates shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, including but not limited to loss of profits, loss of data, loss of business, loss of goodwill, or business interruption, arising out of or in connection with your use of the Website, our services, or these Terms, regardless of the theory of liability (whether in contract, tort, negligence, strict liability, or otherwise) and even if SCIT Consulting has been advised of the possibility of such damages.

In no event shall the total aggregate liability of SCIT Consulting for all claims arising out of or relating to these Terms or the use of the Website exceed the greater of: (a) the total amount paid by you to SCIT Consulting for the specific service giving rise to the claim during the twelve (12) months preceding the event giving rise to the liability, or (b) one hundred euros (EUR 100).

Nothing in these Terms shall exclude or limit liability that cannot be lawfully excluded or limited under applicable law, including but not limited to liability for: death or personal injury caused by negligence; fraud or fraudulent misrepresentation; or any other liability that cannot be excluded or limited under the laws of the Republic of Estonia or applicable European Union law.

You acknowledge that the limitations set forth in this section reflect a reasonable allocation of risk and are a fundamental element of the basis of the bargain between you and SCIT Consulting.

sections.heading: 10. Indemnification
sections.body:
You agree to defend, indemnify, and hold harmless SCIT Consulting, its directors, officers, employees, agents, and affiliates from and against any and all claims, damages, obligations, losses, liabilities, costs, or expenses (including reasonable legal fees) arising from or related to: your use of or access to the Website; your violation of these Terms; your violation of any applicable law, regulation, or third-party right; any content or information you submit or transmit through the Website; or any claim that your use of the Website or our services has caused damage to a third party.

This indemnification obligation shall survive the termination of these Terms and your use of the Website. SCIT Consulting reserves the right, at its own expense, to assume the exclusive defence and control of any matter otherwise subject to indemnification by you, in which event you agree to cooperate with SCIT Consulting in asserting any available defences.

Nothing in this section shall be interpreted so as to impose indemnification obligations that are prohibited or unenforceable under the laws of the Republic of Estonia or applicable European Union consumer protection legislation.

sections.heading: 11. Data Protection
sections.body:
SCIT Consulting is committed to the protection of personal data and fully complies with the General Data Protection Regulation (EU) 2016/679 ("GDPR"), the Estonian Personal Data Protection Act (Isikuandmete kaitse seadus), and all other applicable data protection legislation.

The collection, processing, storage, and transfer of your personal data is governed by our Privacy Policy, which is available at https://scitconsulting.eu/privacy-policy and is incorporated into these Terms by reference. We encourage you to read the Privacy Policy carefully to understand how we handle your personal data.

By using our Website and services, you acknowledge that certain personal data may be collected and processed as described in our Privacy Policy. Where processing is based on consent, you may withdraw your consent at any time without affecting the lawfulness of processing carried out prior to withdrawal.

For any data protection inquiries, requests to exercise your rights under the GDPR (including the rights of access, rectification, erasure, restriction, portability, and objection), or to lodge a complaint, please contact us at privacy@scitconsulting.eu. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the Estonian Data Protection Inspectorate (Andmekaitse Inspektsioon) or any other competent supervisory authority.

sections.heading: 12. Third-Party Links & Services
sections.body:
Our Website may contain links to third-party websites, services, or applications that are not owned or controlled by SCIT Consulting. These include, but are not limited to, Cal.com for consultation scheduling, Botpress for our AI chatbot, and various social media platforms.

SCIT Consulting has no control over, and assumes no responsibility for, the content, privacy policies, practices, or availability of any third-party websites or services. The inclusion of any link on our Website does not imply endorsement, approval, or affiliation with the linked website or its operator.

Your interactions with third-party services, including the provision of personal data, are governed exclusively by the terms and policies of those third parties. We strongly recommend that you review the terms of service and privacy policies of any third-party website or service you access through our Website.

SCIT Consulting shall not be liable for any damage or loss caused or alleged to be caused by or in connection with the use of or reliance on any content, goods, or services available on or through any third-party websites or services.

sections.heading: 13. Termination
sections.body:
SCIT Consulting reserves the right to suspend or terminate your access to the Website, in whole or in part, at any time and for any reason, including but not limited to a breach of these Terms, without prior notice or liability to you.

Upon termination: your right to access and use the Website shall immediately cease; any provisions of these Terms that by their nature should survive termination shall continue in full force and effect, including but not limited to the sections on Intellectual Property, Confidentiality, Limitation of Liability, Indemnification, and Governing Law.

With respect to consulting engagements, termination provisions are governed by the applicable individual service agreement. The termination of your access to the Website does not, in and of itself, terminate any active service agreement, and conversely, the termination of a service agreement does not affect your right to access the publicly available portions of the Website in accordance with these Terms.

If you wish to terminate your relationship with SCIT Consulting or have any questions regarding termination, please contact us at hello@scitconsulting.eu.

sections.heading: 14. Force Majeure
sections.body:
SCIT Consulting shall not be liable for any failure or delay in performing its obligations under these Terms where such failure or delay results from circumstances beyond our reasonable control, including but not limited to: acts of God, natural disasters, or extreme weather events; epidemics, pandemics, or public health emergencies; war, armed conflict, terrorism, or civil unrest; government actions, sanctions, embargoes, or regulatory changes; strikes, labour disputes, or industrial action; failure of telecommunications or information technology infrastructure; cyberattacks, including denial-of-service attacks, ransomware, or data breaches caused by third-party actors; or failure of third-party service providers upon which we rely.

In the event of a force majeure occurrence, the affected party shall promptly notify the other party and shall use reasonable efforts to mitigate the effects of the force majeure event. Performance of the affected obligations shall be suspended for the duration of the force majeure event.

If a force majeure event continues for a period exceeding sixty (60) consecutive days, either party may terminate the affected engagement or these Terms by providing written notice to the other party, without incurring any liability arising from such termination.

sections.heading: 15. Governing Law & Jurisdiction
sections.body:
These Terms and any dispute or claim arising out of or in connection with them or their subject matter or formation (including non-contractual disputes or claims) shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Republic of Estonia, without regard to its conflict of law provisions.

Any dispute arising out of or in connection with these Terms that cannot be resolved amicably shall be submitted to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Tallinn, Republic of Estonia. Both parties irrevocably consent to the personal jurisdiction and venue of such courts.

Notwithstanding the foregoing, if you are a consumer within the meaning of applicable European Union legislation, nothing in this section shall deprive you of the protection afforded by the mandatory provisions of the law of your country of habitual residence, nor shall it restrict your right to bring proceedings in the courts of that country, in accordance with Regulation (EU) No 1215/2012 on jurisdiction and the recognition and enforcement of judgments in civil and commercial matters.

Additionally, if you are a consumer resident in the European Union, you may also make use of the European Commission's Online Dispute Resolution platform, available at https://ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr, for the resolution of disputes arising from online contracts.

sections.heading: 16. Severability
sections.body:
If any provision or part of a provision of these Terms is found to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable by a court of competent jurisdiction, such invalidity, illegality, or unenforceability shall not affect any other provision or the remaining part of the affected provision, and all other provisions shall remain in full force and effect.

In the event that a provision is found to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable, the parties agree that the court shall endeavour to give effect to the parties' original intentions as reflected in the provision, and the invalid or unenforceable provision shall be modified to the minimum extent necessary to make it valid, legal, and enforceable while preserving its original intent. If such modification is not possible, the provision shall be deemed severed from these Terms.

sections.heading: 17. Amendments
sections.body:
SCIT Consulting reserves the right to modify, amend, or update these Terms at any time at our sole discretion. Any changes will be effective immediately upon posting the revised Terms on our Website, with the "Last updated" date at the top of this page reflecting the date of the most recent revision.

For material changes that significantly affect your rights or obligations, we will make reasonable efforts to provide advance notice, which may include posting a prominent notice on the Website, sending a notification to the email address associated with your account or consultation booking, or other appropriate means of communication.

Your continued use of the Website or our services after the posting of revised Terms constitutes your acceptance of the changes. If you do not agree to the revised Terms, you must stop using the Website and our services. We recommend reviewing these Terms periodically to stay informed of any updates.

A historical record of previous versions of these Terms may be requested by contacting us at hello@scitconsulting.eu.

sections.heading: 18. Contact Information
sections.body:
If you have any questions, concerns, or feedback regarding these Terms of Service, please contact us using the information below.

SCIT Consulting OU
Tallinn, Republic of Estonia
Website: https://scitconsulting.eu
General inquiries: hello@scitconsulting.eu
Data protection inquiries: privacy@scitconsulting.eu

We aim to respond to all inquiries within a reasonable timeframe. For urgent matters related to data protection or security, please clearly indicate the urgency in your subject line.
